run.prompt: Please review the uploaded MSA-Helios-Cloud.docx from our position as the CUSTOMER. Give me a customer-protective review of the riskiest clauses, then produce a redlined version of the document with surgical edits that protect us: cap our liability, remove or narrow any unilateral supplier rights, and tighten termination and data-protection terms. I need the redlined .docx saved to this matter.
run.final_answer:
---

## Summary

The redlined document **MSA-Helios-Cloud (redlined).docx** is saved to this matter. Download it and accept/reject each tracked change in Word.

### What was redlined (9 edits, 15 tracked-change regions)

| Clause | Issue | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **§1.3** | "As available" — no SLA | Appended 99.5% uptime commitment, service credits, termination right for <95% in 2 consecutive months |
| **§2.2** | 90-day non-renewal notice — easy to miss | Reduced to 60 days |
| **§3.3** | Charges non-refundable with no exceptions | Added refund exceptions for permitted early termination (material breach, TFC) with pro-rata refund |
| **§4.1** | 24-month hard lock-in, no exit | Replaced with termination for convenience after 12 months, 60 days' notice, pro-rata refund |
| **§4.3** | Suspension for any overdue amount / AUP breach, no cure | 10-day cure for non-payment, disputed amounts excluded, scope limited to affected user, restoration obligation |
| **§5.2** | Liability cap had NO carve-outs beyond death/fraud | Carved out data protection breaches, confidentiality, IP infringement, indemnity obligations, gross negligence/wilful misconduct |
| **§5.3** | Indirect/consequential exclusion had NO carve-outs | Carved out indemnity amounts, data-breach costs (notification, forensic, regulatory fines), confidentiality breach, direct foreseeable damages |
| **§6.3** | Sub-processor changes — 30-day notice, no objection | Added customer right to object on data-protection grounds within 14 days; termination right with refund if objection unresolved within 30 days |
| **§7.2** | Automatic assignment of all feedback, unrestricted | Changed to non-exclusive licence; excludes customer Confidential Information; no obligation to provide |

### Three highest-priority items requiring human judgment

1. **Business leverage.** The redline asks for significant concessions. If this is a low-value deal, you may choose to focus only on the liability carve-outs and let the SLA/termination terms go.
2. **Governing law risk.** The document is governed by English law. Our client (Helios Analytics GmbH) is German. Confirm whether German mandatory rules or GDPR implementation affect enforceability.
3. **Missing foundational protections the redline didn't add** (because they require drafting, not just editing single clauses): a DPA (article 28 contract), a mutual confidentiality clause, mutual warranties, and a defined terms section. These should be added as a second pass if you intend to use this MSA.

steps.summary:
1	---
     2	name: msa-review-saas
     3	description: Use when the user uploads or pastes a Software-as-a-Service Master Services Agreement (MSA), Master Subscription Agreement, SaaS Agreement, or Cloud Services Agreement and asks for review, redline, risk assessment, or recommendation on whether to sign. Conducts a structured review of MSA framework terms (liability, indemnification, IP, data protection, warranties, term and termination, payment, and others), calibrated to the user's perspective (vendor or customer), with severity-rated findings, redline language for material gaps, and clause-level citations. Optionally surfaces conflicts between the MSA and a provided Order Form or SOW.
     4	lq_ai:
     5	  title: MSA Review — SaaS
     6	  version: 1.0.0
     7	  author: LegalQuants
     8	  tags: [contracts, msa, saas, subscription, review]
     9	  jurisdiction: US-default
    10	  trigger_examples:
    11	    - "review this SaaS MSA"
    12	    - "review this software subscription agreement"
    13	    - "redline this cloud services agreement"
    14	    - "what should I watch for in this MSA"
    15	    - "is this SaaS contract okay to sign"

    17	    required:
    18	      - name: document
    19	        type: document

    21	      - name: perspective
    22	        type: text
    23	        description: Which side the user represents. One of "vendor" (the SaaS provider supplying the service; we want strong limitations of liability, broad acceptance of our terms, customer payment obligations, and operational flexibility) or "customer" (the SaaS customer subscribing to the service; we want strong service commitments, controllable termination rights, data protection, IP ownership of customer data, and reasonable liability allocation). If not provided,…

steps.summary:
[MSA-Helios-Cloud.docx (45 pages) — full text]

HELIOS CLOUD LTD — MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT (Vendor Standard Form)
This Master Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is made between:
(1) HELIOS CLOUD LTD, a company incorporated in England and Wales (registered number 09182736) whose registered office is at 40 Helios House, 12 Meridian Street, London EC2A 4TP ("Helios", the "Vendor", "we" or "us"); and
(2) NORTHWIND TRADING LTD, a company incorporated in England and Wales (registered number 07654321) whose registered office is in London ("Customer", "you"),
each a "party" and together the "parties". This Agreement takes effect on the date of last signature (the "Effective Date").
1. PARTIES & SERVICES
1.1 Helios operates a cloud-hosted customer relationship management platform (the "Platform") and shall make the Platform and related support available to the Customer as a service (the "Services") in accordance with this Agreement and the applicable Order Form.
1.2 The Services are provided on a subscription basis for the Customer's internal business use only. Helios grants the Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to access and use the Services during the term. All use is subject to the Platform's published acceptable-use and fair-use policies.
1.3 Helios shall use commercially reasonable efforts to make the Platform available, but the Services are provided on an "as available" basis save as expressly stated in this Agreement.
2. TERM AND RENEWAL
2.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for an initial term of 24 (twenty-four) months from the Effective Date (the "Initial Term").
2.2 On expiry of the Initial Term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive renewal periods of 12 (twelve) months each (each a "Renewal Term"), unless either party gives the other written notice of non-renewal of at least 90 (ninety) days before the end of the then-current term. The Initial Term and each Renewal Term together form the "Term".
3.…
Let me check for any reference files and record what I've learned. [requested tools: glob, record_matter_participant, search_matter_memory]

steps.summary:
{"content_md": "# Helios Cloud MSA — Vendor Review

**Our client:** Helios Analytics GmbH (Munich-based B2B data-analytics SaaS company) — acting as **Customer**

**Counterparty:** Helios Cloud Ltd (UK company), the SaaS vendor

**Document:** MSA-Helios-Cloud.docx — Helios Cloud Ltd's standard-form Master Services Agreement

**Status:** Under review — customer-protective redlining in progress

**Key deal points identified:**
- UK-governed (England & Wales), exclusive jurisdiction
- 24-month initial term, auto-renewing 12-month periods, 90-day notice to prevent renewal
- No SLA / uptime commitment — "as available" basis
- Liability cap: 12 months' fees, no carve-outs beyond statutory minimums
- Broad exclusion of indirect/consequential loss with no carve-outs
- Customer locked in for 24-month Initial Term — no termination for convenience
- Charges non-cancellable, non-refundable
- Feedback/improvements automatically assigned to vendor
- No mutual warranties, no mutual IP indemnity
- Sub-processor: 30-day notice, no customer right to object/terminate
- No DPA referenced (though basic data protection provisions present)
"}

steps.name: update_matter_memory
steps.summary: Updated this matter's memory (1118 characters). It is saved and will be available in future runs on this matter; the prior version was snapshotted so the change can be undone.

# MSA Review — SaaS: Helios Cloud Ltd (Vendor Standard Form)

**Perspective:** Customer (Helios Analytics GmbH)
**Review depth:** Comprehensive
**Document type:** Master Services Agreement (SaaS — cloud CRM platform)
**Governing law:** England and Wales
**Term:** 24-month initial term, auto-renewing 12-month periods, 90-day non-renewal notice
**Order Form provided:** No
**Deal context:** First-time vendor evaluation

---

## Bottom line

This is a short, bare-bones vendor MSA that is **materially unfavorable** to the customer. It is not signable as-is. The document lacks foundational protections for any customer: no SLA or uptime commitment, no termination for convenience during a 24-month lock-in, a liability cap with no carve-outs for the vendor's IP infringement, confidentiality breaches, or data protection failures, and a broad exclusion of indirect/consequential loss. The vendor has asymmetry advantages on suspension, feedback IP assignment, and sub-processor changes. The document needs **significant negotiation** — primarily around liability, termination rights, and service commitments — before it is acceptable.

---

## Critical issues

### 1. No service-level commitment — "as available" (§1.3)

**What it says:**
> "Helios shall use commercially reasonable efforts to make the Platform available, but the Services are provided on an 'as available' basis save as expressly stated in this Agreement."

**Why it's a problem:** There is no uptime commitment, no SLA, and no service credit mechanism. "As available" means the vendor has no contractual obligation to maintain any particular level of service. A customer investing in a 24-month subscription with annual upfront payments has no recourse if the platform is persistently unavailable. For a CRM platform — often business-critical — this is unacceptable.

**Suggested approach:** Delete the "as available" qualifier and introduce uptime commitments (e.g., 99.5% monthly uptime) with service credits and a termination …

If the Customer terminates for convenience under this clause, it shall pay all Charges due up to the effective date of termination, and Helios shall refund any prepaid Charges for the period after the effective date on a pro-rata basis.", "rationale": "The first sentence flatly denies termination for convenience during a 24-month lock-in, which is one-sided. steps.summary:
{"content_md": "# Helios Cloud MSA — Vendor Review

**Our client:** Helios Analytics GmbH (Munich-based B2B data-analytics SaaS company) — acting as **Customer**

**Counterparty:** Helios Cloud Ltd (UK company, reg. 09182736), the SaaS vendor — CRM platform

**Document:** MSA-Helios-Cloud.docx — Helios Cloud Ltd's standard-form Master Services Agreement

**Governing law:** England and Wales — exclusive English courts

**Status:** Reviewed and redlined. Redlined document saved as "MSA-Helios-Cloud (redlined).docx"

## Key terms after redline

| Term | Original | Redlined |
|---|---|---|
| SLA | "as available" | 99.5% uptime, service credits, termination right for sustained failure |
| Term | 24-month lock-in, no early exit | TFC after 12 months, 60 days' notice, pro-rata refund |
| Non-renewal notice | 90 days | 60 days |
| Liability cap carve-outs | None beyond statutory minimums | Data protection, confidentiality, IP infringement, indemnity obligations, gross negligence/wilful misconduct carved out |
| Indirect/consequential exclusion | No carve-outs | Carve-outs for indemnity amounts, data-breach costs, confidentiality breach, direct foreseeable damages |
| Suspension rights | Overdue or AUP breach, immediate | 10-day cure for non-payment (disputed excluded), imminent security risk only, scope limited, restoration obligation |
| Sub-processor changes | 30-day notice, no objection right | Customer may object on data-protection grounds, termination right if unresolved |
| Feedback IP | Automatic assignment, unrestricted | Non-exclusive licence, excludes customer CI, no obligation to provide |
| Charges refund | Non-refundable | Pro-rata refund on permitted early termination |

## Open issues
- No DPA attached (UK GDPR requires article 28 agreement)
- No mutual confidentiality clause (separate from data protection)
- No mutual warranties of authority or service conformance
- No defined terms section
- Docum…

---

## Summary

The redlined document **MSA-Helios-Cloud (redlined).docx** is saved to this matter. Download it and accept/reject each tracked change in Word.

### What was redlined (9 edits, 15 tracked-change regions)

| Clause | Issue | Change |
|---|---|---|
| **§1.3** | "As available" — no SLA | Appended 99.5% uptime commitment, service credits, termination right for <95% in 2 consecutive months |
| **§2.2** | 90-day non-renewal notice — easy to miss | Reduced to 60 days |
| **§3.3** | Charges non-refundable with no exceptions | Added refund exceptions for permitted early termination (material breach, TFC) with pro-rata refund |
| **§4.1** | 24-month hard lock-in, no exit | Replaced with termination for convenience after 12 months, 60 days' notice, pro-rata refund |
| **§4.3** | Suspension for any overdue amount / AUP breach, no cure | 10-day cure for non-payment, disputed amounts excluded, scope limited to affected user, restoration obligation |
| **§5.2** | Liability cap had NO carve-outs beyond death/fraud | Carved out data protection breaches, confidentiality, IP infringement, indemnity obligations, gross negligence/wilful misconduct |
| **§5.3** | Indirect/consequential exclusion had NO carve-outs | Carved out indemnity amounts, data-breach costs (notification, forensic, regulatory fines), confidentiality breach, direct foreseeable damages |
| **§6.3** | Sub-processor changes — 30-day notice, no objection | Added customer right to object on data-protection grounds within 14 days; termination right with refund if objection unresolved within 30 days |
| **§7.2** | Automatic assignment of all feedback, unrestricted | Changed to non-exclusive licence; excludes customer Confidential Information; no obligation to provide |

### Three highest-priority items requiring human judgment

1. **Business leverage.** The redline asks for significant concessions. If this is a low-value deal, you may choose to focus only on the liability carve-outs and let the SLA/terminat…
